instructions: Update the core domain models to support compiler-first context optimization. Introduce a `TargetResolutionMode` Enum (AUTO, STRUCTURE_ONLY, FULL_SOURCE, SCHEMA_ONLY) and add it to the `ContextTarget` Pydantic V2 model. Ensure all models remain strictly typed and frozen.
anti_patterns: Do not import anything from `jitsu.providers` or `jitsu.server` (Domain Driven Design strictness).
Do not break existing Pydantic V2 compatibility.

instructions: Upgrade the `ContextCompiler` to implement the AST-first policy. If a `ContextTarget` uses `AUTO` mode, the compiler should proactively attempt to use the `ast` or `pydantic_v2` provider based on the target type, falling back to `file_state` only if necessary. Add logic to emit a compiled context manifest summarizing what was summarized and omitted.
anti_patterns: Do not execute whole-file dumps if AST or Schema extraction succeeds in AUTO mode.
Do not use `print()` or standard logging; any fatal errors must go to `stderr`.

instructions: Write comprehensive tests for the updated `ContextCompiler` to guarantee 100% test coverage. Specifically test the 'AST preferred over file_state' routing, the fallback to `file_state` on AST failure, and the inclusion of the context manifest in the final markdown payload. Run the `verify-fast` command to confirm strict formatting, linting, and Pyright typing.
anti_patterns: Do not report SUCCESS if code coverage drops below 100%.
Do not use `Any` types in the tests; maintain Pyright strict mode.
